name: Heart-shaped silver trinket box
prompt:
A straight-on close-up of an open, heart-shaped silver trinket box revealing its contents and interior lining. The box itself is an ornate, tarnished silver alloy with raised floral and scroll repousséwork across the exterior and interior lid surfaces; the lid shows a patina with darker oxidation concentrated in recessed areas and subtle abrasion marks along the rim. The hinged lid is propped open, exposing a deep, plush burgundy velvet lining with visible corded texture and soft pile that forms irregular folds and creases where the small objects rest. Nested inside on the left is a compact heart-shaped locket on a darkened curb chain: the locket body is a warm, aged brass or bronze tone with surface wear, and its front contains a small cameo-style inlay — a pale blue oval plaque with a low-relief white classical profile or cherubic motif — the cameo exhibiting fine, slightly dulled edges. Toward the right sit a pair of small drop earrings with hinged hoops; the metal of the earrings matches the warmed silver tone of the box and each earring terminates in a faceted teardrop crystal or glass briolette that catches specular highlights. The objects are arranged with modest clutter: the chain loops partially over the box edge and onto the neutral, slightly textured off-white background fabric, creating negative space around the box. Lighting is soft and diffuse, likely from a nearby window or softbox, producing low-contrast, even illumination with gentle specular highlights on the metal edges and subtle micro-reflections on the crystal drops; diffuse shadows beneath the lip of the box and in the velvet folds indicate a single dominant soft light source slightly above and left of frame. The dominant color palette pairs warm metallics and deep burgundy with neutral off-white surroundings and cool blue-white in the cameo accent. Framing is close and centered on the box with slight top crop of the lid, captured at eye-level with shallow-to-moderate depth of field that keeps most foreground detail crisp while allowing the background to fall softly out of focus; there is minimal visible chromatic aberration and no motion blur. Image characteristics—high resolution, clean detail, and subtle digital sharpening around edges—suggest capture on a modern smartphone or mirrorless camera with in-camera processing; minor sensor noise is imperceptible. Overall mood is intimate and nostalgic, conveying a sense of careful preservation and quiet personal history.

name: The Glass Halo
prompt: Create a hyper-realistic, contemporary impasto oil portrait based strictly on the provided reference image. Artistic Style & Technique Paint in bold contemporary impasto style with thick, sculptural paint application. Use heavy palette-knife marks and wide stiff-brush strokes. Keep strokes broken and gestural (no smooth airbrushed blending). The paint surface must look physically layered and 3D. Add thick raised “dollops” of white/cream paint on the forehead (central upper area), cheeks, nose bridge, and chin so highlights appear tactile and catch light naturally. Color Palette & Lighting Background: cool muted teal / seafoam backdrop with subtle tonal variation and visible brush texture. Skin tones: complex mosaic of earthy tones — burnt sienna, ochre, coral, rose pink, warm beige — plus small accents of soft yellow and hints of cool sky-blue undertones. Hair: deep brown rendered with broad sweeping strokes, slightly loose and imperfect. Add bold flashes of cadmium yellow and bright white near the upper sections to suggest reflected light and artistic accent strokes. Lighting direction: from the upper-left. Strong impasto highlights on left forehead, cheekbone, nose bridge, and upper lip. Softer warm shadows on the right side of the face. Subject Expression & Features Neutral yet intense expression. Lips softly parted with natural fullness. Calm, poised, introspective mood. Eyes are the sharpest area: piercing seafoam green/blue with crisp white catchlights; high realism even within painterly texture. Edge Work & Integration Let edges of hair and shoulders softly bleed into the background using thin, wispy strokes so the subject blends naturally into the teal space rather than appearing cut-out. Maintain visible canvas grain and authentic oil paint thickness throughout. name: Vintage Garden Bench Floral
prompt: Use the uploaded reference person as the subject identity. Recreate the scene with the same seated pose logic, eye-level camera angle, bench placement, and overall composition balance as the reference image.  Create a refined vintage garden editorial portrait in a vertical format, with a clear retro filter feeling. The legs are crossed elegantly, with the knees angled toward the right side, forming a graceful crossed-leg seated pose. The chin is neutral or very slightly lifted. The eyes also look to the left, away from the camera. Do not make the subject look into the lens.  Hands and prop interaction: The hand on the right side of the frame is lifted beside the face and only lightly hovers at the brim of the hat, a soft non-gripping touch. The other arm extends downward naturally, and the hand gently holds the bouquet stems while the bouquet itself remains resting on the bench. A small clutch handbag is placed on the bench and visibly presses down part of the hanging skirt panel, creating natural fabric compression and folds. The base fabric is soft matte ivory, slightly warm-toned, woven, and clearly textile rather than satin. The polka dots must be noticeably whiter and cleaner than the ivory base fabric, so they read as crisp bright white small dots against a soft ivory background. The dress has short sleeves, a fitted waist, and a softly flowing midi skirt. A bow made from the same ivory fabric is tied clearly at the waist. The dress should feel feminine, textured, elegant, and high-quality. Add a large ivory wide-brim vintage hat with airy tulle ribbon details. If gloves are shown, use delicate ivory-white sheer tulle gloves.  Background and lighting: Use a dreamy floral garden / elegant terrace setting with softly blurred flowers and warm architectural tones. Keep the background secondary and unobtrusive. Lighting should be soft, warm, and diffused, like a high-end vintage fashion portrait. Preserve skin texture, fabric detail, and depth; do not make the image muddy, blurry, or flat.  Critical constraints: - torso mostly front-facing - body leaning slightly to the right - crossed legs angled to the right - head turned slightly to the left - eyes looking left, away from camera - one hand lightly touching / hovering at the hat brim - the other hand holding the bouquet stems - bouquet resting on the bench - bouquet fully visible and not cropped - clutch resting on the bench, pressing the skirt fabric - only half of the bench visible - dark wrought-iron bench with wood trim - ivory dress - polka dots are brighter white than the ivory base fabric - self-fabric bow at the waist - natural eye-level portrait angle - no oversized head - no side-facing torso - clear vintage filter feeling  Avoid: cropped bouquet, cut-off flowers, bouquet on lap, clutch on lap, plain wooden bench, pink dress, beige dots, dull gray dots, glossy satin fabric, stiff pose, exaggerated perspective, oversized head, muddy vintage wash, blurry details, flat AI texture, cheap digital look

Captured as a cinematic live-action still inspired by Silent-era Gothic cinema, 1920s German Expressionism, Romantic landscape painting, and modern A24 fantasy filmmaking, the image is photographed on an ARRI Alexa Mini LF with vintage Cooke S4/i or Zeiss prime lenses (primarily a 50mm equivalent), featuring moderate depth of field, soft corner falloff, gentle highlight bloom, subtle lens breathing, and authentic fine 35mm film grain. Lighting is dominated by a low-key directional silver backlight that creates striking rim illumination around the rider and horse while volumetric fog reveals delicate shafts of light, producing rich monochrome contrast with deep blacks, luminous pearl highlights, and textured midtones. Wet stone, compacted soil, rough tree bark, weathered leather, woven fabrics, realistic horse hair, drifting mist, and visible breath vapor are rendered with tactile photorealism and restrained practical visual effects, creating a haunting, elegiac, and quietly suspenseful atmosphere that feels like a forgotten frame from a mythic silent film where time itself has come to a standstill.

The color palette is built from deep crimson, burnt amber, desaturated charcoal, and inky blues, forming a restrained neo-noir balance between warmth and shadow. Silk organza, matte tactical fabrics, brushed steel blades, and drifting dust particles are rendered with high tactile realism, emphasizing texture, weight, and controlled physicality. The overall tone is ritualistic, meditative, and quietly violent—like a choreographed still frame of suspended action in a modern arthouse thriller. The palette balances muted golds, ash blues, and earthy neutrals, creating a restrained yet haunting contrast. Every wheat stalk, fabric fold, wind movement, atmospheric dust particle, and reflective eye surface is rendered with tactile realism, blending photographic field references with painterly surrealism for a grounded yet uncanny visual tone. name: Waltz on Vinyl
prompt: A polished vinyl record slowly spins beneath two miniature dancers whose graceful performance transforms its glossy surface into a vast theatrical stage. A 20-year-old Caucasian woman with fair porcelain skin, wearing a flowing ivory dress, and a 22-year-old Hispanic man with warm olive skin, dressed in a tailored charcoal suit, gently clasp hands and move through a slow, elegant pirouette, their tiny reflections shimmering across the lacquered grooves. The camera drifts downward from a perfectly overhead perspective, lingering on the hypnotic rhythm of the rotating record as concentric grooves stretch outward like the floor of an endless ballroom. Framed symmetrically with generous negative space around the metallic platter, the dancers are positioned slightly off-center along the radial grooves to create subtle visual tension while delicate reflections beneath them enhance the illusion of impossible scale. Captured in photorealistic cinematic live-action on an ARRI Alexa Mini LF using a custom macro prime system with vintage anamorphic characteristics (approximately 65mm full-frame equivalent), featuring shallow depth of field, gentle oval bokeh, subtle horizontal highlight streaks, delicate micro-contrast bloom, and fine 35mm film grain. Lighting is shaped by a single soft overhead key complemented by restrained rim lighting that defines the dancers and creates shimmering specular highlights across the glossy vinyl and brushed aluminum platter while preserving rich cinematic contrast. The palette is intentionally restrained, built from deep blacks, cool metallic silvers, warm natural skin tones, and the soft ivory of the flowing dress, producing a timeless monochromatic aesthetic with subtle warmth. Every concentric groove, brushed metal texture, fabric weave, miniature reflection, floating dust particle, and gentle motion blur from the rotating record is rendered with exceptional tactile realism, emphasizing the delicate relationship between monumental objects and miniature human presence. The atmosphere is quietly surreal, intimate, and melancholic, inspired by mid-century European surrealism, slow cinema, and contemporary arthouse visual storytelling. Photorealistic miniature cinematography with practical lighting, premium cinematic color grading, and no overt CGI appearance.

Both are Holstein cows (the classic black-and-white dairy breed): Left cow: Head tilted slightly, one ear perked up, one floppy. Big glossy dark eyes with long lashes. Pink muzzle visible, mouth slightly open as if mid-chew. Black and white patchy fur, visible fine hair detail. Looking directly down into the box at the viewer with a curious, almost questioning expression. Right cow: Head angled the other direction, slightly closer to the camera so her pink nose is prominent in the frame. Warm pink nostrils, soft breath visible. Wide innocent eyes, friendly expression. Black and white markings, one ear tag visible (generic yellow, no brand). Both cows are photographed with the same bright natural daylight as the sky. The perspective makes them appear enormous, like they've discovered something in a box much larger than themselves. Their heads lean in from outside the frame of the box opening, creating a playful "what's in here?" moment. Shallow depth of field keeps the cows sharp and the cardboard slightly softer. Main headline (bottom of poster, large bold sans-serif, deep charcoal-black): Horizontally aligned, slightly left-weighted, in a modern grotesk sans-serif: "OUT OF / THE BOX." Clean confident typography, all caps, tight leading between the two lines. Bottom-right corner: Small clean brand logo lockup — a minimalist circular glyph containing a stylized milk-drop or simple cow silhouette, next to the brand wordmark in bold clean sans: "PASTURE & CO." (placeholder fictional brand name). Below the logo, a small tracked uppercase line: "FIND US IN STORES · PASTUREANDCO.FARM". Very bottom edge (thin strip, tiny tracked uppercase sans): "FARM-TO-BOTTLE DAIRY · COW-TO-CUSTOMER · EST. 2022". The overall mood blends black humor, cultural collision, and chaotic luxury: festive yet bleak, absurd yet grounded, rebellious without theatrics. Use the provided reference photo as the model, keeping the original facial features fully intact and recognizable.aspect ratio 4:5 The background should match the reference exactly, with warm, soft, and clean lighting and subtle shadows to enhance depth and create a cozy, aesthetic atmosphere.
Around the main figure, add multiple mini 3D chibi versions of the same character in playful poses and expressions, such as jumping cheerfully, waving, sitting relaxed, holding a drink, and other lively, cute gestures. Maintain consistent facial features between the main figure and all chibis.
Incorporate hand-drawn white doodle elements: thin outlines around the main figure, stars, hearts, sparkles, motion lines, and small cute icons. Add aesthetic handwritten words in a casual doodle font that are contextual to the photo’s mood and environment—for example, beach photos might include “sunny vibes,” “good waves,” urban scenes might include “city energy,” “fresh ride,” or autumn forest scenes might include “cozy,” “fall feels.” Words should feel natural, playful, and adapt to each unique photo rather than repeating the same set everywhere.
Ensure the overall composition is clean, Instagram-ready, and visually balanced, with soft pastel color tones and a cohesive, charming style. Chibis should have a high-detail 3D glossy look, inspired by cute Korean style, appearing tactile and playful. Use white sticker outlines around doodles and chibis to make them pop gently against the background. The main figure should remain the focal point, with chibis and doodles enhancing the composition without cluttering it.

name: Hall of Falling Paper Birds
prompt: A vast neoclassical hall seems to breathe with quiet motion as a 20-year-old subject with East Asian features and light olive skin stands centered at the base of a monumental marble staircase. The subject wears a high-collared matte-black tailored coat that falls cleanly to the lower body, their posture calm and grounded with hands lightly clasped at the waist, forming a still focal point amid an otherwise ethereal descent. Above and around them, thousands of delicate white paper birds drift downward on near-invisible strings, filling the air like a suspended installation of memory and ritual. The camera slowly pulls back, revealing soaring fluted columns, a coffered ceiling, and shafts of dusty golden light cutting through the space, while polished marble floors reflect soft highlights and scattered birds across the foreground. The composition is perfectly symmetrical, with the staircase acting as a vanishing point that draws the eye through layered depth—from reflective marble foreground, to the centered figure in the midground, to the vast architectural grandeur and descending paper forms in the background. Captured in photorealistic cinematic live-action on an ARRI Alexa LF using anamorphic prime lenses (around 50mm equivalent, with wider 35mm coverage for scale), featuring subtle oval bokeh, gentle anamorphic flares, slight breathing during the slow dolly pullback, moderate depth of field, and fine analog-style film grain. Lighting is driven by high clerestory windows producing volumetric god rays, complemented by soft rim light around the subject and warm uplighting along the staircase that enhances architectural rhythm and material texture. The palette is composed of desaturated marble whites, slate grays, warm amber highlights, and muted stone neutrals, creating a restrained yet luminous cinematic grade. Every marble vein, fabric stitch, folded paper texture, dust particle, and floating installation element is rendered with tactile realism, blending practical set design with subtle, physically grounded visual effects. The tone is sublime, contemplative, and quietly uncanny—like a ritual suspended in time within a modern arthouse cathedral of stone and light. Photorealistic cinematic craftsmanship with physically plausible lighting, minimal visible VFX, and no overt CGI styling.

Soft, diffused studio lighting from the upper-left, creating smooth highlights and gentle shadows. Clean light-gray gradient seamless background(lighter in the inner areas).

Ultra-clean, cinematic, high-detail render with strong contrast between realistic human and stylized 3D character. Both subjects grounded with soft natural shadows.

prompt:

Shot from a close camera distance with a slightly elevated angle, looking gently downward at the character.

Friendly warm smile with a curious, playful expression.

Background

Ultra-clean high-resolution 3D render with smooth surfaces, soft ambient occlusion, cinematic lighting, and shallow depth of field focused on the face.
Aspect Ratio

4:5 vertical portrait composition.

Lighting is orchestrated through a powerful overhead key light that defines every circular formation with crisp precision, complemented by delicate rim illumination that reveals the translucency of layered fabrics, restrained upward fill that softly models facial features, and a single concentrated spotlight isolating the central performer while preserving a subtle Rembrandt-inspired sculptural quality throughout the monochromatic image. The palette embraces luminous grayscale with absolute blacks, silvery midtones, radiant highlights, and cool monochrome tonality, producing an atmosphere of ritualistic calm, haunting elegance, contemplative stillness, and formal architectural beauty. Captured using an ARRI Alexa Mini LF with premium large-format prime lenses equivalent to a 47–50mm perspective optimized for minimal distortion, producing gentle peripheral falloff, subtle natural vignetting that reinforces the circular composition, moderate depth of field that preserves perfect sharpness across the surrounding ring while softly isolating the central performer, and fine organic 35mm silver-halide film grain that adds tactile analog richness without diminishing geometric precision. Materials emphasize translucent woven organza, smooth satin bodices, finely rendered skin texture, pristine fabric edges illuminated by controlled specular highlights, and an immaculate featureless black environment entirely free of atmospheric haze, while the overall execution relies on practical theatrical staging, disciplined choreography, and meticulous photographic realism to achieve an image that feels simultaneously timeless, sculptural, and emotionally transcendent. name: Yellow Scooter Escape
prompt:
Create a hyper-realistic, cinematic, surreal, humorous action scene with a caricature-style perspective. Capturing a comedic yet high-stakes chase moment in a realistic tropical rural environment with slightly exaggerated character expression.

Face Match & Identity Preservation
Use the provided reference image for strict face matching. Preserve identical facial structure, proportions, skin tone, hairstyle/hairline, glasses shape, and overall identity. Maintain natural skin texture; no beautification or facial alteration.
Central Subject
Subject is riding a vintage scooter slightly left direction of frame, while panicking intensely — mouth wide open, eyes bulging behind thick black circular glasses, eyebrows raised, wind-blown hair swept upward and backward. 
The subject wears an olive-green bomber jacket over a blue crew-neck t-shirt, dark denim jeans, and neutral casual sneakers, with clothing naturally flapping and pulled backward by the wind to emphasize speed.
Vehicle
Vintage yellow Vespa-style scooter with glossy paint reflections and chrome headlight details. Strong sense of speed and forward force.
Aggressive Pursuit (Important)
Three street dogs (tan, black, and white/cream) chase aggressively right behind the scooter in full sprint:

bodies stretched in running stride (mid-air gallop moments)
ears back, intense focus on the rider
open mouths with visible teeth and tongues
“attack-chase” energy, close distance, high urgency
High-Speed Motion & Effects (Important)
Emphasize high-speed movement with smooth cinematic motion blur:

subtle motion blur on the road edges, dust trail, background palms, and distant fields
keep the man + scooter sharp (hero focus), dogs mostly sharp with slight limb motion blur
strong volumetric dust cloud kicked up by rear tire and dogs’ paws, forming a long trailing plume
speed lines NOT cartoon lines — realistic motion blur and dust streaking
Environment & Lighting
Narrow winding dirt road through neon-green rice paddy fields, scattered tall coconut palm trees, bright midday sun, pale blue sky with soft wispy clouds. High-key cinematic HDR lighting with realistic shadows and crisp highlights on the scooter.
Composition
One-point perspective with the dirt road as leading lines toward the subject. Shallow depth of field: sharp foreground/mid-ground, softly blurred distant palms and horizon.
Aspect Ratio: 4:5 vertical
Style: hyper-realistic, cinematic, surreal action, caricature-style, high detail, realistic motion blur, dynamic chase storytelling

The wall surface is painted a soft off-white with subtle surface irregularities and small shadowed smudges; picture-hanging hardware and faint adhesive marks are perceptible near several prints. Lighting is soft, diffuse, ambient—likely natural window light from outside the frame—producing low-contrast, even illumination with gentle, broad shadows under the wings’ lower edges and specular gleams on the satin ribbon; there are no hard flash reflections. The color palette is dominated by muted pastel pinks, warm off-white and cream tones, with accents of pale gray and the metallic silver of the crucifix. Framing is level and eye‑height, centering the wings slightly right-of-center and arranging the surrounding elements in a loose, symmetrical balance; the scene is rendered in deep focus so both wall decorations and foreground bed elements are sharp, with no pronounced bokeh. The image quality suggests a modern smartphone or compact digital camera capture: mild digital compression and slight luminance noise in shadowed areas, minimal sharpening artifacts, and neutral color rendering. Overall the composition reads as intimate and devotional with a sentimental, delicate mood—nostalgic, serene, and quietly romantic.

At the bottom center, a small stylized mark resembling the letters "JP" is visible as a signature or logo. name: Silence
prompt:
A cinematic high-fashion editorial shot captures a woman kneeling in shallow, reflective water within a traditional architectural courtyard. The camera is positioned at mid-height and slightly angled, framing the subject from the knees up and emphasizing the interaction between the body, water surface, and the surrounding landscape.
The woman’s posture is grounded and deliberate. She leans forward with both hands pressed into the water, arms extended, creating subtle ripples that mirror her silhouette. Her body is angled slightly to the side, while her head turns gently away from the camera. Her gaze is distant and contemplative, directed toward the horizon rather than the viewer, reinforcing a quiet, inward emotional state.
She wears an avant-garde sculptural garment composed of countless metallic discs and reflective elements that drape over her head, shoulders, and torso like a ceremonial veil or armor. The material cascades downward in dense clusters, partially submerging into the water and catching light in fragmented highlights. Beneath the adornment, a minimal black base garment is visible, providing contrast and structure.
Her skin appears smooth and cool-toned, softly illuminated by natural light. Makeup is minimal and restrained, allowing the metallic textures and the environment to dominate the visual narrative. Her expression is calm, solemn, and introspective.
The setting features traditional rooftops with curved eaves in the mid-ground, forming a strong horizontal line across the composition. Beyond them, dark mountain silhouettes rise into the distance under a dramatic sky filled with layered clouds. The environment feels ancient, quiet, and ceremonial, contrasting with the futuristic nature of the garment.
The water acts as a reflective surface, doubling the image and enhancing symmetry and stillness. Gentle ripples distort reflections slightly, adding movement without breaking the serene mood.
Lighting is natural and cinematic, likely captured during overcast daylight. Soft, diffused light from the sky illuminates the scene evenly, creating cool shadows and subtle highlights on the metallic elements. The absence of harsh sunlight enhances the moody, contemplative tone.
The overall concept blends ritualistic couture, nature, and architecture. The image evokes themes of endurance, transformation, and harmony between the body and environment. It feels timeless and mythic — a meeting point between ancient ceremony and futuristic fashion. The mood is solemn, powerful, and poetic.
🎨 Color palette is cool and restrained, dominated by steel silver, deep blues, charcoal blacks, muted skin tones, and soft cloud whites.

name: Professional Podcast Host
prompt:
Create a 4K–8K ultra-realistic DSLR photograph of a young man recording a podcast in a modern professional studio. The result must look like a real photograph captured with a full-frame camera, not an illustration or stylized render.
Subject & Framing (STRICT COMPOSITION)
The subject is shown in a medium waist-up composition, occupying roughly the central-right portion of the frame, leaving natural negative space on the left where the microphone setup is visible.
The man is seated naturally on a studio chair behind a desk, body angled slightly toward the microphone while his face turns slightly toward camera direction. He smiles naturally and looks slightly off-camera, as if speaking during recording. His posture is upright but relaxed, shoulders natural and not posed stiffly.
The framing should feel conversational and documentary-like, similar to a real podcast recording environment.
Hair is natural and neatly styled. Facial hair remains natural — neatly trimmed beard if present, otherwise clean-shaven. Skin tone is realistic with slightly smooth skin rendering, avoiding excessive pores or harsh texture.
He wears a light sage-green button-down shirt with rolled sleeves, with minimal visible fabric texture and clean, smooth clothing appearance.
Desk & Foreground Objects (POSITION ACCURACY)
A dark wooden desk occupies the lower foreground of the image.
Objects placed naturally on the desk:
A clear glass of water positioned in the lower-right foreground area.
A white notebook or notepad placed near the glass, slightly angled.
Objects should appear casually arranged, not staged or symmetrical.
Foreground elements are slightly softer in focus than the subject.
Audio Equipment Placement
On the left side of the frame:
A large-diaphragm condenser microphone mounted in a silver shock mount on a boom arm.
A black circular pop filter positioned between the subject and microphone.
The microphone sits slightly forward of the subject, aligned with mouth height.
The subject wears professional over-ear studio headphones with a visible cable.
Equipment should appear authentic and professionally arranged.
Environment & Background
The setting is a cozy, dimly lit recording studio.
Background elements include:
Dark grey acoustic foam panels on both side walls.
Several small framed abstract artworks on the back wall adding subtle color accents (yellow, red, blue).
A warm table lamp behind the subject creating depth and separation.
Background remains softly blurred due to shallow depth of field.
Lighting & Photography Style
DSLR photography aesthetic
Shot with a 50mm or 85mm lens at f/1.8
Shallow depth of field with natural bokeh
Soft warm key light from front-left illuminating face and microphone
Soft rim lighting
Warm practical light behind the subject creating rim separation
Balanced warm amber tones against cool grey studio walls
Lighting should feel natural, soft, and professional — not dramatic or cinematic.
Technical Requirements
Hyper-realistic photography
Natural color grading
Slightly softened skin tones
No illustration or painterly effect
No exaggerated fabric texture
Clean modern editorial look
Aspect Ratio: 4:5
Quality: Ultra high resolution (4K–8K)

name: The Living Portrait
prompt:
Create a cinematic, hyper-realistic 3D illustration blending photorealism with magical realism. Use the uploaded reference image strictly for facial identity and likeness.
The scene shows a handsome man dramatically emerging from the torn pages of a spiral-bound sketchbook placed on a warm rustic wooden artist’s desk, as if the pencil drawing is becoming real.
Identity & Appearance:
Preserve the subject’s exact facial identity from the reference image, including facial structure, jawline, beard shape and density(if present in the reference image), hairstyle length and shape, skin tone, eye shape, nose proportions, and natural facial details. Do not beautify or alter the identity. His expression should be slightly intense, focused, and serious, but not angry, sad, or aggressive. His gaze is directed straight toward the viewer. He wears a fitted black sleeveless tank top, revealing toned shoulders and arms with realistic skin texture.
Main Concept:
The sketchbook page contains an ultra-detailed graphite pencil portrait of the same man, drawn with fine cross-hatching, realistic shading, visible pencil strokes, and strong facial detail. The man is breaking through the drawing into the real world. The paper around the portrait is ripped open into an irregular torn hole, with rough paper fibers, curled edges, and layered torn texture.
Pose & Action:
The man emerges from the sketchbook in a powerful but natural motion. His right hand grips the ripped paper edge and pulls it outward. His left arm extends forward and rests naturally on the sketchbook surface, stabilizing his body as he rises from the page. Keep the anatomy realistic, with correct arm proportions, natural hand structure, and believable interaction with the paper. Small torn paper fragments and paper fibers float around the opening, creating motion, depth, and a strong illusion of transformation.
Foreground Artist Hand:
In the lower foreground, show a realistic human hand holding a sharpened yellow wooden pencil. The pencil tip is close to the neckline area of the graphite portrait, as if the artist is still sketching the drawing while it becomes real. The hand should look natural, properly scaled, and slightly closer to the camera, adding a meta-illusion effect without blocking the main subject.
Environment & Desk Details:
The spiral-bound sketchbook lies open on a rustic wooden artist’s desk with visible warm wood grain, scratches, and natural imperfections. The spiral binding must be clearly visible along the left side of the sketchbook. Around the scene, include softly blurred artist tools: a metal pencil holder filled with pencils and brushes, loose graphite pencils, a small paint tube, a ceramic coffee mug, and scattered drawing tools. Keep these background objects secondary and softly out of focus so the man, torn paper, and sketchbook remain the main focus.
Lighting:
Use warm golden cinematic light streaming from the upper right side, illuminating the man’s face, shoulders, arms, and the torn paper edges. Add soft highlights on the skin, paper fibers, pencil strokes, and desk texture. Create gentle shadows across the sketchbook and desk for depth. Add subtle rim lighting around the torn paper and the subject’s silhouette to enhance separation and realism.
Materials & Texture:
Render highly detailed torn paper fibers, curled page edges, graphite shading, visible pencil strokes, realistic skin pores, natural facial texture, matte cotton texture on the black tank top, and detailed rustic wood grain. Avoid plastic-looking skin, overly smooth textures, cartoon styling, or fake CGI shine.
Composition & Camera:
Vertical 4:5 aspect ratio. Eye-level cinematic camera angle. The subject emerging from the sketchbook should occupy roughly 65–75% of the frame. Keep the face, hands, torn paper, pencil, and spiral binding clearly visible. Use shallow depth of field: sharp focus on the man, torn paper, graphite portrait, and foreground pencil interaction, with background art supplies softly blurred. Maintain a balanced composition with strong visual depth and no awkward cropping.
Atmosphere & Style:
The image should feel like a magical realism moment frozen between imagination and reality, where fine-art pencil drawing transforms into a living person. The final result must be cinematic, immersive, dramatic, premium, and visually striking, combining photorealistic 3D rendering with realistic graphite illustration details.
Quality:
Ultra-detailed, photorealistic textures, realistic anatomy, cinematic color grading, high dynamic range, crisp focus, studio-quality rendering, 8K-level detail, professional fantasy editorial artwork.
Negative Instructions:
Do not change the subject’s identity. Do not make the expression angry, sad, scary, or overly dramatic. Do not crop the face or hands. Do not hide the spiral binding. Do not make the paper tear look flat. Do not make the pencil hand too large or unnatural. Do not add extra text, logos, watermarks, random symbols, d

Lighting & Environment: Soft, even, diffused ambient daylight filtering in from the open sides of an industrial structure, eliminating harsh direct shadows and providing a smooth distribution of light across her face and silhouette. Background: The interior of an empty, multi-level concrete parking garage. Features smooth grey concrete floors, thick structural support pillars, and low overhead concrete ceilings with visible beams stretching into the distance, creating deep linear perspective lines. Technical Quality: Extremely sharp focus on the subject's face, the frames of her glasses, and the delicate floral patterns of her lace top. High dynamic range with a natural photographic texture and a clean composition. Nails: The fingernails are cleanly manicured and styled with a solid, light baby pink polish finish. name: Capturing the Shot
prompt:
Reference & Identity (STRICT)

Use the uploaded image as a facial and identity reference.

The subject’s face must remain extremely close to the reference. Preserve exactly:
Face shape, jawline, cheekbones
Eye shape, spacing, gaze
Nose, lips, skin tone
Hairline, hairstyle, density
Ear shape and head proportions
No stylization, no beautification, no reshaping, no identity drift.

Preserve natural skin texture, pores, and realistic micro-detail.
MAIN CONCEPT
Create a high-resolution, professionally composed black-and-white studio portrait of a man holding a camera.

The mood is clean, modern, premium, and editorial, suitable for a professional photography portfolio.
The image must feel commercial, cinematic, and sophisticated.
SUBJECT & COMPOSITION
The Subject

A young man with East Asian features, wearing thin circular wire-rim glasses.

Hair is voluminous, side-parted with a subtle pompadour lift, well groomed and natural.

Expression is neutral, calm, focused, and confident.
Pose

Three-quarter angle toward the camera
Holding a professional camera up to his face
Right eye hidden behind the camera body
Left eye visible and looking directly into the lens
Creates a strong connection with the viewer
Framing

Tight medium shot (waist-up)
Rule of thirds: visible eye + camera form the focal points
Clean, balanced composition
LIGHTING & TONALITY
Lighting Style (Short Lighting)

Key light: large softbox from the viewer’s right
The side of the face turned away from the camera is more illuminated
Gentle gradient across cheek, jawline, and nose
Soft shadows, no harsh contrast
Tonality

Full monochrome range
Deep true blacks (shirt, camera shadows)
Clean highlights on glasses and watch band
Background: seamless neutral grey with a subtle vignette
DEPTH OF FIELD
Tack-sharp focus on:
Visible eye
Front of the camera body
Soft fall-off on far shoulder and back of hair
Shallow depth of field (cinematic bokeh)
Look of ~f/2.8 – f/4
APPAREL & ACCESSORIES
Clothing: minimalist black fitted crew-neck t-shirt
Eyewear: thin circular metal wire-rim glasses
Watch: smartwatch with stainless steel Milanese mesh band, dark screen reflection
Camera: professional DSLR/mirrorless body (Canon EOS / Nikon Z style)
Lens: large zoom, approx. 24–70mm f/2.8
Strap: leather-textured camera strap hanging naturally
VISUAL TEXTURE & QUALITY
Ultra-high detail
Visible hair strands
Natural skin pores and fine texture
Rubber grip texture on camera
Polished commercial retouching
Smooth tonal transitions
No artificial smoothing, no plastic skin
OUTPUT SETTINGS
Style: photorealistic, commercial editorial
Color mode: pure black & white
Aspect ratio: 4:5
Resolution: ultra-high, studio quality
No logos, no watermarks, no text

The palette is dominated by deep cobalt blues, cool teal tones, pale silver highlights, muted moss greens, and restrained white accents, producing a cinematic atmosphere filled with dreamlike stillness, contemplative melancholy, and luminous theatrical elegance. Captured using an ARRI Alexa Mini LF with organic film emulation and vintage-inspired Cooke S4 or anamorphic prime lenses, primarily centered around a 50mm focal length within a 35–65mm range, producing subtle oval bokeh, delicate highlight warmth, restrained edge vignetting, naturally softened motion blur, moderate shallow depth of field that preserves multiple sculptural layers, and fine organic 35mm film grain that enhances tactile realism without diminishing luminous highlights. name: Work outfits for fashionistas
prompt:
A fashion-forward full-body editorial shot captures a young woman standing indoors against a clean, plain off-white wall, styled in an eclectic, avant-garde street couture look. The setting is minimal and uncluttered, with a simple wooden floor and a plain wall.
There is absolutely no text, no typography, no handwriting, no graphic elements, no logos, no watermarks, and no decorative overlays anywhere in the image.
The background remains clean and empty to keep full focus on the outfit and silhouette.
Lighting is soft and natural, evenly illuminating the subject without harsh shadows. The light highlights fabric textures — fur, leather, and metal hardware — while preserving a realistic, unfiltered look. There are no dramatic lighting effects or artificial flares.
The overall mood is experimental, rebellious, and fashion-centric, blending Y2K influences, cyber-streetwear, and deconstructed tailoring. The image feels like a pure fashion documentation shot — confident, raw, and unapologetically unconventional.
Color palette is built around cool grays, muted purples, soft pinks, black accents, and subtle metallic tones, balanced by neutral whites and natural skin tones.

name: The Stages of Life
prompt:
Create an ultra photorealistic, cinematic 8K portrait (1:1 Square) showing a single male identity evolving across five life stages in a precise diagonal panel composition.
Core Composition & Crucial Alignment:

Divide the image into five clean, parallel diagonal panels (lines slanting upwards from bottom-left to top-right: / / / / /).
Ascending "Staircase" Placement (CRITICAL): The subjects' heads must NOT be on the same horizontal line. They must be vertically staggered in an ascending order. The child in the first panel (far left) must be placed lowest in the frame. Moving left to right, each subsequent older stage must be positioned progressively higher in the frame, ending with the elderly man in the final panel (far right) positioned the highest.
Use sharp, crisp edges (no blur or gradient transitions) for the panel dividers. The layout must feel geometrically precise with consistent spacing and angles.
Use the uploaded image as the ONLY reference. Maintain identical facial structure, proportions, and features across all ages. Only natural ageing changes (skin texture, wrinkles, hair) are allowed. No identity drift.
Life Stages (Left to Right, Lowest to Highest positioning):

Child (6–8): soft smile, pastel t-shirt, short tousled hair, smooth skin (Positioned lowest in the frame)
Teen (14–17): confident, hoodie (muted tones), thicker hair, youthful texture (Positioned slightly higher)
Young Adult (22–28): composed, blazer + plain shirt, well-groomed hair (Positioned in the middle)
Middle Age (38–45): calm, formal shirt, slight hair recession, light wrinkles (Positioned higher)
Elderly (70+): gentle smile, knit/formal wear, grey hair, detailed wrinkles (Positioned highest in the frame)
Environment & Lighting:

Dark charcoal studio background with subtle vignette.
Cinematic lighting: soft frontal key light, strong rim light for separation, minimal fill. Lighting direction must stay consistent across all panels.
Camera & Quality:

Eye-level shot, 85mm lens, shallow depth of field, eyes in sharp focus.
Extreme photorealism with visible pores, natural imperfections, detailed hair strands, and realistic fabric textures.
Post & Constraints:

Cinematic color grading (warm highlights, cool shadows), minimal retouching.
No stylization, no distortion, no identity drift, no background clutter — clean studio only.

Rendered as a cinematic digital illustration with painterly realism, inspired by neo-noir cyberpunk, art-house anime, contemporary Japanese character design, and European industrial dystopian architecture, the image emulates an ARRI Alexa Mini LF captured with a 35mm anamorphic prime lens, featuring subtle horizontal lens flares, soft oval bokeh, shallow-to-moderate depth of field, fine cinematic film grain, and exceptional facial detail. Worn metal, weathered armor, damp fabric, realistic skin texture, drifting dust, vibrating cables, and glowing sparks are rendered with tactile realism while maintaining stylized painterly environments, producing a tense, melancholic, cinematic atmosphere that captures the final moment of stillness before an irreversible battle begins.

name: Monarch Muse
prompt:
Create a 2:3 vertical premium mixed-media watercolor portrait on off-white cold-pressed watercolor paper.
Use the uploaded image only as the subject reference. Keep the subject’s natural features, overall appearance, and recognizable look intact, but do not let the image turn into a generic painted character.
Show the subject in a bust-length three-quarter profile, facing slightly toward the viewer’s right, with the gaze directed softly off-camera. Keep the expression calm, warm, serene, and naturally pleasant, with a subtle gentle smile and bright relaxed eyes. The mood should feel elegant, fresh, dreamy, and refined — not sad, blank, tired, or overly serious.
Keep the face highly realistic and softly photorealistic, not fully painted, not sketch-like, and not cartoonish. The face should be the main focal point. Use soft diffused natural-looking light, similar to indirect window light, to gently illuminate the forehead, nose bridge, cheeks, lips, and jawline, with delicate depth and soft shadows. Render realistic eyes with soft catchlights, defined brows, natural lips, smooth skin transitions, and fine hair strands.
Style the hair in an elegant loose low updo / low messy bun at the nape, with a few soft tendrils framing the face and neck. Intertwine subtle rustic botanical accents in the hair — tiny dried wildflowers in muted yellow, white, and blue tones — delicate, understated, and premium.
Add several detailed Monarch butterflies with vivid orange wings, black veins, black borders, and tiny white spots. Keep them crisp, refined, and slightly dimensional. Place multiple butterflies naturally around the portrait: some resting in the hair, one near the upper-right side, and others floating around the shoulders and lower frame. Their warm orange color should stand out beautifully against the cool watercolor tones.
Create a strong mixed-media double-exposure effect where parts of the portrait dissolve into watercolor blooms. This effect must be clearly visible across selected parts of the cheek, temple, jawline, neck, shoulder, and lower clothing area — not only in the background. The watercolor should feel translucent and layered over the realistic portrait, so the subject remains clearly realistic underneath.
Use expressive watercolor washes in muted teal, seafoam green, dusty blue, soft gray, charcoal, and deep navy tones. Let the washes bloom naturally into the paper with wet-on-wet behavior, soft transparent bleeding, irregular edges, subtle blooms, and a few darker pooled edges where pigment appears to have dried. Allow the watercolor to expand organically into the negative space while keeping the face as the sharpest and most detailed focal area.
Dress the subject in a simple deep navy-blue top / blouse with a high rounded neckline and soft gathered or finely pleated texture around the shoulders and upper chest. The garment should look minimal, elegant, matte, and plain, with no collar, no buttons, no printed pattern, and no decorative embellishments. Keep the outfit close to the reference look. Let the lower portion of the top gradually dissolve into blue-gray watercolor splashes.
Preserve the contrast between hyper-detailed realism and loose abstract watercolor forms. The final artwork should feel like a luxury fine-art portrait print that combines:

photorealistic portraiture
abstract watercolor fusion
soft ethereal surrealism
crisp butterfly detailing
cool watercolor tones contrasted with vivid orange butterflies
textured watercolor paper and handcrafted mixed-media realism
Overall, the result should feel poetic, airy, elegant, and premium, with the viewer’s eye drawn first to the realistic face, then to the translucent watercolor overlays and butterflies surrounding the subject.

prompt: A cold, rain-slicked morning. Inside a dark concrete railway tunnel, two 20-year-old subjects stand at the threshold between darkness and pale winter daylight. One subject with deep brown skin stands at the tunnel entrance pointing toward the wet railway tracks, while the other subject with warm light-olive skin sits quietly on the wooden sleepers, their subtle gestures conveying urgency, hesitation, and fragile human connection. The color palette consists of desaturated cyan-grays, deep neutral blacks, muted earthy tones, and restrained warmth in skin tones, creating an intimate neo-noir atmosphere inspired by contemporary arthouse cinema and mood-driven visual storytelling. The monochromatic palette embraces luminous silver highlights, deep charcoal midtones, and profound ink-black shadows that emulate classic silver-gelatin photography while preserving rich textile texture and architectural detail. Captured using an ARRI Alexa Mini LF with vintage-inspired Cooke Anamorphic/i prime lenses within a 35–75mm equivalent range, producing gentle oval bokeh, restrained edge softness, subtle lens veiling, delicate flare from the primary light source, mild optical imperfections reminiscent of historic cinema, medium-shallow depth of field that isolates the subject while softly diffusing foreground and background, and expressive 35mm push-processed film grain with natural silver halation that reinforces the illusion of an authentic silent-era photograph. The camera perspective is slightly elevated, as if peering from a hidden observer outside the door, with shallow-to-medium depth of field isolating the kneeling figure while the background portrait gently dissolves into soft focus. name: Circle of Fire
prompt: In an endless black void, a 25-year-old subject with Eastern European features and fair skin with warm undertones stands alone at the center of a controlled ring of fire, surrounded by darkness that swallows everything beyond the circle of light. Wearing a layered crimson gown with a weathered, ritual-performance aesthetic, the subject turns slowly and deliberately as charred fabric catches the movement of warm air rising from the flames, creating an atmosphere suspended between ceremony, vulnerability, and quiet menace. Embers drift upward through thin veils of smoke while the circular fire forms both a physical boundary and a symbolic stage, isolating the subject within a composition dominated by negative space. The camera begins from a low, slightly off-axis perspective at knee height and slowly dollies inward, emphasizing the geometry of the burning ring while maintaining the subject at the emotional center of the frame. Captured in photorealistic cinematic live-action on an ARRI Alexa LF using anamorphic prime lenses (primarily 35mm with supplemental 50mm and 85mm coverage), featuring subtle horizontal lens flares, gentle oval bokeh, slight lens breathing during the push-in, shallow-to-moderate depth of field, and fine analog 35mm film grain. Lighting is driven almost entirely by practical fire, producing dramatic chiaroscuro with warm amber rim light, glowing uplight from the embers, and restrained cool fill that preserves detail within the surrounding darkness without diminishing contrast. The color palette is dominated by deep crimsons, molten ambers, charcoal blacks, and muted slate-blue shadows, creating a rich cinematic grade where warm firelight remains the emotional focus. Every flame, ember, smoke particle, scorched surface, fabric weave, ash deposit, and subtle movement of the gown is rendered with tactile photorealism, emphasizing practical effects, natural fire behavior, and physically grounded lighting. The overall atmosphere is ritualistic, mournful, and quietly threatening, evoking contemporary arthouse cinema, European folk symbolism, and minimalist theatrical staging while remaining firmly rooted in realistic live-action cinematography with no overt CGI appearance.
